title: IAM, Bucket Policies, Access Points, and Tenant Isolation · Type Forensics
summary: A Node incident packet contains a plausible least-privilege TypeScript authorization proof with one unsafe assertion, stale object identity, unbounded stream, lost rejection, abort leak, cache defect, policy gap, or recovery overclaim; preserve the symptom and isolate first cause. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in least-privilege TypeScript authorization proof and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory…
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, evaluate identity and resource policies by principal, action, exact resource, condition, session, explicit deny, and organization boundary.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, enforce Block Public Access and bucket-owner-enforced Object Ownership while explaining what each does and does not authorize.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, derive tenant-safe keys and list prefixes server-side and bind allowed actions and conditions without trusting user-supplied policy fragments.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Principal Action Resource Conditi…
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 4294 during a browser upload compatibility rollout. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to evaluate a runtime-validated explicit-deny-aware tenant authorization record; bound bytes, time, attempts, promises, streams, concurrency, cache, and spend; then replace one typed fixture with unknown runtime input and prove rejection before AWS use. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, evaluate identity and resource policies by principal, action, exact resource, condition, session, explicit deny, and organization boundary.

Inspect the Principal Action Resource Condition trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Principal Action Resource Conditi…, keep Principal Action Resource Condition connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Policy Simulation Negative…
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: storage-ts-policy-simulation-negative-tests-13fy2n" that uses case 9584 to evaluate a runtime-validated explicit-deny-aware tenant authorization record. Incident diagnosis: preserve the symptom, isolate first cause, and prove a regression case before repair. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node or AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, networks, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the TypeScript 7.0.2 native compiler and the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 compiler that reports 6.0.3, Node 24.18.0, AWS SDK v3.1087.0, controlled AWS, stream and handle, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.
